possible_interpretations.text: The three paths may represent one archival family with original, access, and preservation roles, because the declared relationships link both derivatives to the same original.
The proposed names may express a shared identity through the dc.identifier prefix "NA-0001" and the descriptor "campana-poster," while retaining separate role directories and suffixes.
The descriptor appears associated with the Spanish title "Campaña poster" and the source stem "campaña-poster."

possible_meaning_loss.text: Changing "campaña" to "campana" removes the tilde distinction between ñ and n. In Spanish, this can alter reading or lexical meaning rather than merely changing typography.
The proposed access and preservation names likewise replace "campaña" with "campana," so the same distinction is lost across all three family members.

decision_card.uncertainty: The evidence establishes the source and proposed spellings and the declared family roles, but it does not state whether preserving the Spanish ñ in filenames is an archival requirement or whether an ASCII-only operational form is required.
decision_card.why_the_distinction_matters: Treating the change as technical normalization emphasizes filename consistency, while treating ñ as identity-bearing language information emphasizes fidelity to the Spanish title and source name. That distinction affects discoverability, human interpretation, and whether the paths continue to reflect the recorded wording.
decision_card.discriminating_question: Must the filename descriptor preserve the Spanish ñ as identity-bearing text, or must it use the supplied ASCII form "campana"?

candidate_explanations.explanation: This candidate corresponds to the declared original, adds the dc.identifier prefix and an explicit original-role suffix, and changes the source descriptor from "campaña-poster" to "campana-poster."

This candidate corresponds to the declared access derivative, retains the access directory, adds the dc.identifier prefix, expresses the access role with a double-underscore suffix, and changes "campaña" to "campana."

This candidate corresponds to the declared preservation derivative, retains the preservation directory, adds the dc.identifier prefix, expresses the preservation role with a double-underscore suffix, and changes "campaña" to "campana."
